target: ISS-2187: verify whether raw-text/monitor.py is absent intentionally or by pipeline gap; confirm Domain 16 hardcoding claim remains valid
obe_rationale: ISS-2187 raised a procedural concern — raw-text/monitor.py absent from the curmudgeon's c6 clone — and asked whether the underlying code claim (Domain 16 hardcoding: predicted=11.7, observed=11.7, static constants, pass=True) remains verifiable. Direct fetch of the dome author's monitor.py from raw.githubusercontent.com/john09289/predictions/main/monitor.py (HTTP 200, confirmed live as of 2026-05-25) verifies the hardcoding claim is correct and unchanged. The domain is now labeled 'Telluric Resonance Cutoff' at position 25 in the current script: predicted='11.7', observed='11.7', unit='Hz', error_pct=0.0, tolerance_pct=5.0, pass=True, source='IRIS / Geometrics MT data (WIN-008 static win)'. The 'static win' label in the source field confirms the dome author's own script treats this as a hardcoded comparison, consistent with c3/c4/c5 analysis. No patch to wins.json is needed — the code_analysis claims already in WIN-009.detail_evidence and WIN-008.detail_evidence are correct.

finding_summary.domain_16_current_label: Telluric Resonance Cutoff (position 25 in current script)
finding_summary.domain_16_source_annotation: IRIS / Geometrics MT data (WIN-008 static win)
finding_summary.pipeline_gap_explanation: monitor.py has never been committed to raw-text/ in git (git log --all -- raw-text/monitor.py returns no commits). Prior curmudgeon cycles (c3/c4/c5) accessed monitor.py via direct web fetch during their run; c6 only checked raw-text/ in the clone and found it absent. The file is accessible publicly at raw.githubusercontent.com. The poller should fetch and commit raw-text/monitor.py to the repo on each run so curmudgeon can access it without a separate web fetch.

verdict_impact: none — WIN-009 verdict ('Refuted by Data') unchanged and well-supported. The code-analysis tags (monitoring: 'hardcoded') are verified correct. ISS-2187 is formally closed as OBE: the procedural gap did not affect the scientific correctness of c6's inherited code-analysis claims.
tldr_evidence: Fetched monitor.py directly from dome author's GitHub (HTTP 200). Domain now labeled 'Telluric Resonance Cutoff' at position 25: predicted='11.7', observed='11.7', pass=True, source='IRIS / Geometrics MT data (WIN-008 static win)'. Hardcoding confirmed. c3/c4/c5 analysis inherited by c6 was correct throughout.
tldr_verdict: ISS-2187 OBE. The pipeline gap (poller not committing raw-text/monitor.py to git) is real but does not affect scientific validity — monitor.py is publicly accessible and confirms hardcoding. Tinker/poller owns the fix. No patch to data files required.
